response: John Wesley founded Methodism along with his brother Charles and fellow cleric George Whitefield. John Wesley was a key figure in the revival movement in England and was central to the Great Awakening in the United States. Charles Wesley, his brother, was also a prominent revivalist and theologian, and George Whitefield was a famous evangelist who played a significant role in the spread of Methodism. Together, they were instrumental in establishing Methodism as a movement.
